source.title: Phasic excitation of dopamine neurons in ventral VTA by noxious stimuli
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Juxtacellular labelling lets the authors anchor electrophysiological response patterns to confirmed dopaminergic identity AND to anatomical position within VTA — both rare and methodologically strong. The finding that ventral-VTA DA neurons are phasically excited by footshock (the opposite of the textbook RPE picture) is a real qualifier on Model 1, but it is also the result that motivates the value/salience split rather than overturning the value-coding part.
failure_signatures: An REE relief-completion mechanism that simply 'reads dopamine concentration' as the teaching signal will mis-learn — ventral-VTA DA fires for the *aversive event itself*, not just for its offset.
Does not record during aversive *offset* directly; the inference about relief comes from combining this with later work, not from this paper alone.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Juxtacellularly labelled dorsal-VTA dopamine neurons in anaesthetised rats are inhibited by noxious footshock (consistent with the value-coding / RPE picture). Juxtacellularly labelled ventral-VTA dopamine neurons are phasically excited by the same stimulus. Two anatomically distinct dopamine systems exist within VTA.
mapping.ree_translation: The naive Model 1 reading — 'phasic dopamine = good thing happened, polarity-flexible at input' — is too coarse for what the substrate would have to instantiate. Some VTA dopamine neurons fire for the aversive event itself, which means a downstream tag-and-release circuit that just listens to a pooled DA signal cannot distinguish 'pain just happened' from 'pain just stopped'. REE needs to either (a) restrict the relief-completion teaching signal to the value-coding DA population specifically, or (b) gate the tag-write on a comparator (suffering-state-change-derivative) rather than on raw DA firing.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Anaesthetised preparation; the precise behaviour of these populations during awake aversive-offset is inferred from later work rather than tested here. The 'two types' partition is sharper than awake-behaving recordings sometimes show.
mapping.source_context: Anaesthetised Sprague-Dawley rats; juxtacellular recording and labelling of single DA neurons in ventral and dorsal VTA during noxious footshock.

confidence_components.notes: Juxtacellular labelling is the gold standard for confirming dopaminergic identity at the single-cell level, which is unusual rigor for an electrophysiology paper. The transfer concern is mainly the anaesthetised preparation.
